title: artemisia lactiflora var. lactiflora
intro: Artemisia lactiflora var. lactiflora, also known as White Mugwort, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Chinese and Ayurvedic practices, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating menstrual cramps, and aiding in digestion. White Mugwort can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
